topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts that address various aspects of federal funding, appropriations, and accountability in government operations.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of an increase in the guarantee fee required to be charged by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury from selling any outstanding shares of senior preferred stock until Congress passes specific legislation.
Document 2: This bill amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act to authorize advance appropriations for the Indian Health Services.
Requires the President's budget to include detailed estimates related to the advance appropriations.
Document 3: Prohibits any person with a seriously delinquent tax debt from obtaining a federal government contract or grant.
Requires federal agency heads to certify that prospective contractors do not have such a debt.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to make grants to increase the capacity of community-based organizations.
Directs the President to include a report regarding federal financial support for community economic development.
Document 5: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in specific water treatment projects.
Limits the federal share of each project's costs to 25%.
Document 6: Amends the sentencing guidelines for crack cocaine offenses to apply only to cases pending on and after a specified date.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers a tax deduction for qualified private school expenses.
Disallows any amount claimed as home school tuition.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments to the Social Security Act aimed at improving Medicare and Medicaid services and coverage.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to the use of average sales price methodology for calculating payments for drugs and biologicals under Medicare part B.
Excludes from the manufacturer's average sales price when calculating such payments any customary prompt pay discounts extended to wholesalers.
Document 2: Integrity in Medicare Advanced Diagnostic Imaging Act of 2011 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to exclude certain advanced diagnostic imaging services from the in-office ancillary services exception to the prohibition on physician self-referral.
Prohibits a physician from referring a patient to the entity for certain advanced diagnostic imaging services for which a Medicare payment otherwise may be made.
Document 3: Breast Cancer Recovery Improvement Act - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to require payment for post-mastectomy external breast prosthesis garments regardless of whether such items are supplied to the beneficiary before or after the mastectomy.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop policies to ensure appropriate beneficiary access and utilization safeguards for such items.
Document 4: Optometric Fairness in Medicaid Act of 2009 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require Medicaid coverage of professional medical services of optometrists.
Document 5: Removing Barriers to Colorectal Cancer Screening Act of 2014 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to waive coinsurance for colorectal cancer screening tests.
Covers 100% of their cost under Medicare part B.
Document 6: Start Healthy Act of 2013 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to provide mandatory coverage of qualified (uninsured) newborns.
100% federal medical assistance percentage (FMAP) for temporary coverage of such newborns.
Document 7: Medicare Access to Rehabilitation Services Act of 2007 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to repeal the outpatient therapy cap.

Document 4: directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states, high-need local educational agencies (LEAs), or consortia of high-need LEAs that partner with external entities to improve access to, and the sharing and use of, education data.
Document 5: directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to review, update, revise, and publish the Radon Measurements In Schools Guidelines.
Document 6: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand the programs eligible to participate in federal student aid.
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive, matching, five-year grants to high-need local educational agencies that partner with public or private nonprofit organizations to assist schools in implementing innovative secondary school reform strategies.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures related to immigration, refugee admission, and visa categories in the United States.

Document 2: This bill permits an alien applying for U.S. refugee admission who is referred for resettlement by the United Nations High Commissioner on Refugees (UNHCR) to be admitted only after the Department of the Treasury...
Document 3: This bill requires that, before the U.S. refugee admission of a covered alien, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shall ensure that such person has passed a lie detector test.
Covered alien means an alien applying for U.S. refugee admission who: (1) is a national of Iraq or Syria.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make certain nonimmigrant investors (E-2 visa) who have resided in the United States for at least five years immediately eligible to adjust to permanent resident status.
Document 5: This bill prohibits the U.S. refugee admission of an alien who is a national of, has habitually resided in, or is claiming refugee status due to events in any country containing terrorist-controlled territory.
Such an alien may be admitted to the United States as a refugee if the alien satisfies refugee admission requirements.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to subject an individual with direct hiring authority who knowingly hires an illegal alien to the following criminal penalties.

Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust to conditional permanent resident status up to 50,000 aliens who have earned a master's or doctorate degree in a science, technology, engineering, or mathematics field.

Document 2: Helping Families Save their Homes in Bankruptcy Act of 2009 - Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor

Document 3: Cap the GSE Bailout Act of 2011 - Limits the funds that may be provided to the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae)

Document 4: FHA Housing Stabilization and Homeownership Retention Act of 2008 - Amends the National Housing Act (NHA) to create the Refinance Program Oversight Board

Document 5: Expanding Refinancing Opportunities Act of 2012 - Amends the National Housing Act to authorize the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to insure or commit to insure any mortgage made to refinance an eligible mortgage

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at protecting personal data privacy and enhancing security protocols for sensitive information.

Document 1: Notification of Risk to Personal Data Act of 2007 requires notification of security breaches involving personally identifiable information.
Exempts agencies from notification requirements for national security and law enforcement purposes.
Document 2: Protect Our Health Privacy Act expands requirements for reports on breaches of unsecured protected health information.
Requires the Attorney General to report annually to Congress on wrongful disclosure of health information.
Document 3: Consumer Privacy Protection Act mandates notice to consumers before using their personally identifiable information for unrelated purposes.
Establishes privacy policy requirements for entities handling consumer data.
Document 4: Amends federal criminal code to require consumer consent for disclosing personally identifiable information by video tape service providers.
Document 5: Personal Data Privacy and Security Act enhances penalties for identity theft and unauthorized access to personal data.

Document 6: Protecting Education Privacy Act amends FERPA to prevent unauthorized release of student educational records without parental consent.
Requires breach notification policies for educational institutions.
Document 7: Student Privacy Protection Act mandates information security practices to protect students' education records.

topic.name: Federal Reserve Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the accountability, transparency, and regulatory framework of the Federal Reserve System.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to require the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to consider specified economic impacts of any regulation before issuing it.
Requires the Board to make publicly available on its website staff salaries and financial information.
Document 2: Requires the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to make the value of the U.S. dollar equal to price of gold on the exchange operated by the Commodities Exchange.
Prohibits the Board, in regulating the value of the U.S. dollar, from conducting open market operations indirectly.
Document 3: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to extend the date of issuance for certain regulations regarding the swaps and security-based swaps market.
Establishes the Office of Derivatives within the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to administer SEC rules governing security-based swaps.
Document 4: Establishes a framework for a market-based trigger to monitor and regulate the adequacy of bank capital of those bank holding companies whose total consolidated assets are $50 billion or more.
Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to notify a bank holding company whose average daily closing price exceeds either 50 basis points, or 75 basis points.
Document 5: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to increase the asset threshold under the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement.
The bill also exempts from the internal control attestation requirements of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) a 'covered agency' that may share information with another covered agency.
Prohibits information submitted to the CFPB in the course of its supervisory or regulatory process from being construed as waiving any privilege.
Document 7: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions.
Prescribes minimum leverage ratios for tier 1 capital maintained by a bank holding company or financial company.
